title: Twitter and ChatGPT blackout blamed on ‘unacceptable’ failure
detail:
Twitter and ChatGPT blackout blamed on ‘unacceptable’ failure
Matthew Field
Tue, November 18, 2025 at 10:16 AM CST 8 min read
In this article:
NET
Websites were hit by issues with Cloudflare - AP Photo/Eric Risberg

Twitter and ChatGPT have been knocked offline in an internet blackout blamed on the “unacceptable” failure of a “routine” upgrade at technology giant Cloudflare.

Internet users began reporting problems loading X, previously known as Twitter, around midday on Tuesday. Users of dozens of other apps and websites, including artificial intelligence chatbot ChatGPT, also reported widespread issues.

The crash has been pinned on problems at Cloudflare, an internet infrastructure business that provides cyber security and networking services to hundreds of major websites. The disruption lasted around three hours.

A Cloudflare spokesman said the company was investigating a “spike in unusual internet traffic” shortly before the issues emerged.

However, an executive later admitted the fault was caused by a “latent bug” in its systems that led to a mass internet crash after a “routine configuration change”. Cloudflare confirmed the problem was not caused by a cyber attack.

Dane Knecht, Cloudflare’s chief technology officer, apologised for the massive internet glitch and the time it took to fix, calling the blackout “unacceptable”.

He said: “The sites, businesses, and organisations that rely on Cloudflare depend on us being available and I apologise for the impact that we caused.”

The internet blackout comes just weeks after problems with Amazon’s internet infrastructure division, Amazon Web Services, knocked swathes of the web offline in the largest crash in history.

04:11pm

‘No evidence’ blackout was down to cyber attack, Cloudflare says

A Cloudflare spokesman has offered more detail on today’s blackout - confirming the outage was not down to a cyber attack.

The spokesman said: “The root cause of the outage was a configuration file that is automatically generated to manage threat traffic. The file grew beyond an expected size of entries and triggered a crash in the software system that handles traffic for a number of Cloudflare’s services.

“To be clear, there is no evidence that this was the result of an attack or caused by malicious activity. We expect that some Cloudflare services will be briefly degraded as traffic naturally spikes post incident but we expect all services to return to normal in the next few hours.”

That’s all for today - thank you for joining our coverage.


03:39pm

Cloudflare executive blames bug in ‘routine’ update for mass blackout

A top Cloudflare executive has blamed a “latent bug” in its systems for a mass blackout that took swathes of the internet offline this afternoon.

Dane Knecht, Cloudflare’s chief technology officer, said the worldwide internet crash was not down to a cyber attack, but was down to a problem introduced when it attempted to make “routine” updates to its service.

Story Continues

title: Cloudflare experiences severe outage, spreads to exchanges
detail:
Cloudflare experiences severe outage, spreads to exchanges
Anand Sinha
Tue, November 18, 2025 at 9:52 AM CST 2 min read
In this article:
NET

Cloudflare Global Network has been experiencing a severe outage, which has affected popular crypto platforms such as BitMEX, DeFiLlama, etc.

On Nov. 18, Cloudflare said it is experiencing an internal service degradation which has impacted some services. It is working to restore services, the web security firm added.

Related: Smartest man alive reveals new Bitcoin target

The BitMEX crypto trading platform also got caught in the storm and suffered disruption. DeFiLlama, the on-chain analytics platform, and Arbiscan, the Abritrum block explorer, also went down due to the Cloudflare outage.

The Cloudflare (NYSE: NET) stock fell approximately 4% to close at $202.25 yesterday. It was exchanging hands at $195.35 in pre-market hours, down 3% in a day.

TheStreet Roundtable reached out to Cloudflare for a comment and has not received a response so far. We will update the story if and when the company responds.

More News:

Popular crypto company shuts down as Bitcoin crashes

CoinMarketCap, Reserve launch index token offering top 20 crypto exposure

Bitcoin miner alleges Stripe cut them off as Trump bans crypto debanking

Crypto community reacts

A large number of crypto users found it difficult to access the social media platform X and the AI chatbot ChatGPT, among other platforms. Both these platforms are widely used by crypto users for discussions and information gathering.

Members of the crypto community who could access X reacted to the outage in an amusing manner.

For instance, I_ChenCrypto complained, "Irony: the 'decentralized' world held together by a single DNS provider."

Another user, @chocoo_web3, wrote, "Can they change crypto price while at it?"

The disappointment isn't unfounded as the total crypto market cap dropped to $3.12 trillion, down 3% in the last 24 hours.

Bitcoin (BTC) fell as low as $89,300.46 a few hours ago and was trading at $91,467.75 at the time of writing. Ethereum (ETH) also fell below the $3,000 price mark, currently trading at $3,045.58. XRP is exchanging hands at $2.17, lying in the red zone despite the launch of the first spot XRP ETF in the U.S.

Related: XRP ETF surges to $26M in opening volume within 30 minutes

This story was originally reported by TheStreet on Nov 18, 2025, where it first appeared in the Real Time Problem & Outage News section. Add TheStreet as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

title: Websites down after outage at network firm Cloudflare
detail:
Websites down after outage at network firm Cloudflare
Henry Saker-Clark
Tue, November 18, 2025 at 9:29 AM CST 2 min read
In this article:
NET

Websites including X, ChatGPT and Spotify (SPOT) have been hit by a major outage linked to Cloudflare.

Thousands of users reported issues with a host of different websites, including the film review site Letterboxd, which were impacted by technical issues at the internet network services business.

Multiplayer games such as League of Legends and the Scottish Parliament website have also all been impacted.

The DownDetector monitoring site, which was itself hit by the outage, showed a flurry of reported issues after 11am on Tuesday.

Grab from the DownDetector.co.uk website following the outage (DownDetector.co.uk/PA)

More than 10,000 DownDetector users reported issues related to Cloudflare.

A number of the websites affected, including X, came back online temporarily before suffering further problems.

Users saw a message on a number of the websites saying the issues were caused by an “internal server error on Cloudflare’s network”.

Cloudflare provides network and security services for many online businesses in order to help their websites and applications operate.

The company said in a server update that it was “experiencing an internal service degradation” and that some service may be intermittently affected.

It added: “We are seeing services recover, but customers may continue to observe higher-than-normal error rates as we continue remediation efforts.”

Cloudflare said it has now restored dashboard services but is still working to “remediate broad application services impact”.

It said it had to temporarily disable some services for UK users as it attempts to rectify multiple outages early on Tuesday.

Users have also reported issues with Shopify, Dropbox, Coinbase, and the Moody’s credit ratings service.

Cloudflare, which runs services such as checking that visitors to websites are humans rather than bots, says around a fifth of all global websites use some of its services.

Cloudflare had scheduled maintenance for the SCL (Santiago) data centre for Tuesday.

Alan Woodward, professor of cybersecurity at the University of Surrey said: “The downside of being a gatekeeper and distribution network for such big brands is that if this vital system fails, no one can use your service be that website or app.

“It’s still not clear exactly what went wrong but it looks like it was a technical malfunction within Cloudflare network.

“This in itself is surprising as such networks are designed to avoid single points of failure.”

title: Global Cloudflare outage interrupts services at X, ChatGPT
detail:
Global Cloudflare outage interrupts services at X, ChatGPT
Cloudflare said it is working on a fix after identifying an issue and was forced to disable some features for those in the United Kingdom. (Credit: Jason Alden/Bloomberg)
Ben Cousins
Tue, November 18, 2025 at 8:14 AM CST 1 min read
In this article:
NET

A global outage at internet infrastructure provider Cloudflare has interrupted services at X, ChatGPT and several other services.

Cloudflare said it is working on a fix after identifying an issue and was forced to disable some features for those in the United Kingdom.

“We have made changes that have allowed Cloudflare Access and WARP to recover,” the company wrote on its status page.

”Error levels for Access and WARP users have returned to pre-incident rates. We have re-enabled WARP access in London. We are continuing to work towards restoring other services.”

Nearly 1,000 users reported an outage on X just before 7 a.m. ET, according to Downdetector.com.

Outages like Amazon's cloud services bound to repeat: experts

Issues still affect AWS service after fixing widespread outage

Others reporting issues include Shopify Inc., Dropbox, Coinbase, online game League of Legends, Moody’s and NJ Transit.

With files from The Associated Press

title: X, Spotify and ChatGPT among those hit by major outage
detail:
X, Spotify and ChatGPT among those hit by major outage
Sky News
Tue, November 18, 2025 at 6:21 AM CST 2 min read
In this article:
NET

Social media site X, music streaming service Spotify and AI chatbot ChatGPT were among several platforms down for thousands of users.

Facebook, AWS (Amazon Web Services), bet365, Canva, BrightHR, and the multiplayer game League of Legends also suffered outages, according to Downdetector.com.

More than 10,000 people reported issues linked to Cloudflare, a web infrastructure company, on Tuesday morning.

The issues were first reported around 11am, with several websites, including X, coming back online temporarily before suffering further problems attributed to an "internal server error on Cloudflare's network".

Cloudflare, which provides network and security services for many online businesses in order to help their websites and applications operate, says around a fifth of all global websites use some of its services.

The company said in a server update that it was "experiencing an internal service degradation" and that some services may be intermittently affected.

It added: "We are seeing services recover, but customers may continue to observe higher-than-normal error rates as we continue remediation efforts."

At 1.09pm, Cloudflare said it had identified the issue and was implementing a fix.

The outage coincides with Cloudflare's scheduled maintenance at its SCL (Santiago) data centre on Tuesday.

Graeme Stuart, head of the public sector at Check Point, a cybersecurity firm credited with creating the first firewall, said: "Cloudflare going down today sits in the same pattern we saw with the recent AWS and Azure outages. These platforms are vast, efficient and used by almost every part of modern life."

"When a platform of this size slips, the impact spreads far and fast and everyone feels it at once," he explained.

Read more from Sky News:
Paris's Louvre closes gallery weeks after heist
Hacker ordered to pay back over £4m in Bitcoin

Mr Stuart said the reported outages did not happen because each organisation failed on its own, but because "a single layer they all rely on stopped responding".

"Many organisations still run everything through one route with no meaningful backup. When that route fails, there is no fallback. That is the weakness we keep seeing play out," he said.

"The internet was meant to be resilient through distribution, yet we have ended up concentrating huge amounts of global traffic into a handful of cloud providers."

title: Looking at the Cloudflare Narrative as Analyst Outlooks Shift on Growth and Partnerships
detail:
Looking at the Cloudflare Narrative as Analyst Outlooks Shift on Growth and Partnerships
Simply Wall St
Tue, November 18, 2025 at 3:16 AM CST 5 min read
In this article:
NET

Cloudflare’s consensus analyst price target has edged upward from $232.49 to $239.35, reflecting an improved outlook among Wall Street analysts. This increase follows strong third-quarter results and a surge in optimism around Cloudflare’s ability to capture growth, particularly among large enterprise customers and strategic product lines. Stay tuned to discover how investors can track these evolving forecasts and stay ahead as the Cloudflare narrative continues to shift.

Stay updated as the Fair Value for Cloudflare shifts by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Cloudflare.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

Recent analyst updates for Cloudflare reflect increased conviction in the company’s growth trajectory and execution, with several price targets raised following strong third-quarter results. Below is a summary of the latest commentary from Wall Street analysts, illustrating both bullish and cautious viewpoints.

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

KeyBanc raised its price target to $300 from $235, emphasizing Cloudflare’s accelerating growth rate, which exceeded 30% year-over-year for the first time in over a year. The firm highlighted the company’s strong execution and multiple positive earnings beats as major positives.

Truist increased its target to $275 from $230, citing momentum among Cloudflare’s largest customer cohort, impressive go-to-market execution, and the success of SASE, Zero-Trust, and AI product lines. Truist noted Cloudflare’s unique position at the intersection of AI, security, and the evolving web ecosystem.

RBC Capital lifted its price target to $265 from $250, underscoring Cloudflare’s 31% revenue growth and reiterating confidence in both near-term performance and long-term opportunity.

Citizens JMP moved its target up to $270 from $250, calling out "impressive" Q3 results and reinforcing the outlook for long-term capital appreciation.

Analysts commonly reward Cloudflare for robust execution, continued top-line momentum, and expanding product adoption.

However, some bullish analysts acknowledge that valuation remains a consideration and that much optimism is being priced in, with near-term risks not entirely absent.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Scotiabank raised its price target to $255 from $200 but maintained a more reserved Sector Perform rating. The analyst cited extensive fieldwork indicating limited current adoption of Cloudflare's SASE and edge compute products among CIOs and CISOs, signaling caution despite the company's broader progress.

UBS increased its target modestly to $245 from $240 and kept a Neutral stance, acknowledging standout Q3 performance but noting a balanced risk-reward outlook going forward.

Bearish commentary focuses on reservations around valuation, the extent to which growth and upside are already reflected in the price, and some uncertainty regarding wider product adoption in the enterprise market.

title: 2 Cash-Producing Stocks Worth Investigating and 1 That Underwhelm
detail:
2 Cash-Producing Stocks Worth Investigating and 1 That Underwhelm
2 Cash-Producing Stocks Worth Investigating and 1 That Underwhelm
Anthony Lee
Mon, November 17, 2025 at 10:45 PM CST 3 min read
In this article:
NET
SFM
WBD

While strong cash flow is a key indicator of stability, it doesn’t always translate to superior returns. Some cash-heavy businesses struggle with inefficient spending, slowing demand, or weak competitive positioning.

Cash flow is valuable, but it’s not everything - StockStory helps you identify the companies that truly put it to work. That said, here are two cash-producing companies that excel at turning cash into shareholder value and one that may struggle to keep up.

One Stock to Sell:
Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD)

Trailing 12-Month Free Cash Flow Margin: 10.9%

Formed from the merger of WarnerMedia and Discovery, Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) is a multinational media and entertainment company, offering television networks, streaming services, and film and television production.

Why Should You Sell WBD?

Annual sales declines of 5.1% for the past two years show its products and services struggled to connect with the market

Suboptimal cost structure is highlighted by its history of operating margin losses

Performance over the past five years shows its incremental sales were much less profitable, as its earnings per share fell by 32.2% annually

Warner Bros. Discovery is trading at $22.76 per share, or 6.6x forward EV-to-EBITDA. To fully understand why you should be careful with WBD, check out our full research report (it’s free for active Edge members).

Two Stocks to Watch:
Cloudflare (NET)

Trailing 12-Month Free Cash Flow Margin: 10.4%

With a massive network spanning more than 310 cities in over 120 countries, Cloudflare (NYSE:NET) provides a global network that delivers security, performance and reliability services to protect websites, applications, and corporate networks.

Why Will NET Outperform?

Billings growth has averaged 34.2% over the last year, indicating a healthy pipeline of new contracts that should drive future revenue increases

Expected revenue growth of 27.5% for the next year suggests its market share will rise

Software platform has product-market fit given the rapid recovery of its customer acquisition costs

At $202.12 per share, Cloudflare trades at 28.7x forward price-to-sales. Is now a good time to buy? Find out in our full research report, it’s free for active Edge members.

Sprouts (SFM)

Trailing 12-Month Free Cash Flow Margin: 5.3%

Playing on the secular trend of healthier living, Sprouts Farmers Market (NASDAQ:SFM) is a grocery store chain emphasizing natural and organic products.

Why Are We Positive On SFM?

Fast expansion of new stores to reach markets with few or no locations is justified by its same-store sales growth

Same-store sales growth averaged 7.7% over the past two years, showing it’s bringing new and repeat shoppers into its stores

Revenue outlook for the upcoming 12 months is outstanding and shows it’s on track to gain market share

title: Cisco acquires translation startup EzDubs
detail:
Cisco acquires translation startup EzDubs
Ivan Mehta
Mon, November 17, 2025 at 12:33 PM CST 2 min read
In this article:
CSCO
REPI.PVT
NET
Image Credits:Carol Yepes / Getty Images

Networking giant Cisco has acquired EzDubs, a Y Combinator-backed consumer startup that provides real-time translation services. Cisco announced the acquisition over the weekend, but the company didn’t disclose the size of the deal.

EzDubs was founded by Padmanabhan Krishnamurthy, Amrutavarsh Kinagi, and Kareem Nassar in 2023. Notably, Nassar had previously worked at Cisco’s Speech AI group before co-founding the startup. The company raised $4.2 million in seed funding led by Venture Highway, which was founded by Neeraj Arora, who was previously WhatsApp’s chief business officer. Other investors in EzDubs include Replit CEO Amjad Masad; Replit President Michele Catasta; Qasar Younis, who is CEO of the autonomous vehicle software company Applied Intuition; and Ben Firshman, CEO of the cloud startup Replicate (which was itself acquired by Cloudflare in a deal that was announced on Monday).

Cisco said it plans to integrate EzDubs’ tech into its communication platform, Cisco Collaboration, which spans hardware and software. This means users can get features like live translation in products like Webex video calling and messaging. Cisco’s blog post also indicated that it could make the translation tech — which preserves the speaker’s original voice — available to partners and developers.

“The EzDubs team will join Cisco Collaboration, working side-by-side with our product, engineering, and go-to-market teams. Together, we will chart a new course for the industry, one where AI doesn’t just support collaboration, but truly empowers it,” Snorre Kjesbu, SVP of Collaboration at Cisco, said in the post.

Cisco didn’t clarify whether all members of EzDubs’ team are joining the company. We have asked the company to clarify and will update the story if we hear back.

EzDubs is sunsetting its consumer apps, which supported call translations for more than 30 languages, by December 15.

“From launching the world’s first video dubbing tool that garnered millions of views on X (fka Twitter) to enabling real-time, voice and emotion preserving phone call translation across 30+ languages, this journey has been extraordinary. But what has truly meant the most is the support, feedback, and stories you’ve shared along the way,” the startup said in a blog post.

In recent months, there have been a few acquisitions in the translation space. This month, Seven Seven Six-backed Palabra AI acquired live communication platform Talo. In July, language localization company TransPerfect acquired Unbabel, a translation startup founded in Portugal.

EzDubs’ acquisition also raises the question of whether it’s viable to build a translation focused on consumers when there is more money and demand in the enterprise communication market. According to various reports, the translation services market is valued at approximately $40 billion.

title: Cloudflare, Inc. (NET) Is a Trending Stock: Facts to Know Before Betting on It
detail:
Cloudflare, Inc. (NET) Is a Trending Stock: Facts to Know Before Betting on It
Zacks Equity Research
Mon, November 17, 2025 at 8:00 AM CST 5 min read
In this article:
NET

Cloudflare (NET) has been one of the most searched-for stocks on Zacks.com lately. So, you might want to look at some of the facts that could shape the stock's performance in the near term.

Over the past month, shares of this web security and content delivery company have returned +0.3%, compared to the Zacks S&P 500 composite's +1.5% change. During this period, the Zacks Internet - Software industry, which Cloudflare falls in, has lost 11%. The key question now is: What could be the stock's future direction?

Although media reports or rumors about a significant change in a company's business prospects usually cause its stock to trend and lead to an immediate price change, there are always certain fundamental factors that ultimately drive the buy-and-hold decision.

Revisions to Earnings Estimates

Here at Zacks, we prioritize appraising the change in the projection of a company's future earnings over anything else. That's because we believe the present value of its future stream of earnings is what determines the fair value for its stock.

We essentially look at how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to reflect the impact of the latest business trends. And if earnings estimates go up for a company, the fair value for its stock goes up. A higher fair value than the current market price drives investors' interest in buying the stock, leading to its price moving higher. This is why empirical research shows a strong correlation between trends in earnings estimate revisions and near-term stock price movements.

Cloudflare is expected to post earnings of $0.27 per share for the current quarter, representing a year-over-year change of +42.1%. Over the last 30 days, the Zacks Consensus Estimate has changed +175%.

For the current fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $0.9 points to a change of +20% from the prior year. Over the last 30 days, this estimate has changed +21.2%.

For the next fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $1.17 indicates a change of +29.4% from what Cloudflare is expected to report a year ago. Over the past month, the estimate has changed +6.4%.

With an impressive externally audited track record, our proprietary stock rating tool -- the Zacks Rank -- is a more conclusive indicator of a stock's near-term price performance, as it effectively harnesses the power of earnings estimate revisions. The size of the recent change in the consensus estimate, along with three other factors related to earnings estimates, has resulted in a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) for Cloudflare.

GREENWICH, Conn., Nov. 17, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Leverage Shares by Themes is thrilled to unveil nine new single stock leveraged ETFs, available for trading starting November 17, 2025. These products are designed to empower investors to amplify returns (up and down) and actively engage with the performance of influential companies.

The new ETFs are tailored to target a 200% exposure to the daily performance of their underlying stocks, offering sophisticated traders and the retail investor efficient tools to help capitalize on market movements at an industry-low management fee for single stock leveraged ETFs at .75%.*

The new ETFs are:

NUG – Leverage Shares 2X Long NU Daily ETF [NuLegacy Gold Corp.]

As part of the Leverage Shares by Themes offering, these new funds aim to provide investors with amplified exposure to a range of potentially high-growth innovators across industries. These funds bring the total count of Leveraged Single Stock Daily ETFs at Leverage Shares by Themes to 43.
